rationale: Title X is more informative and more publishable for Heart Rhythm style because it states the main discriminating finding, the pulmonary vein isolation relevance, and the study design. The colon structure is acceptable and the title has a stronger clinical electrophysiology cadence. Title Y is understandable, but it reads more generic and method focused, with weaker phrasing around the finding.
loser_weakness: Title Y is less polished: "as a quantitative marker to" makes the title longer without adding much, "nearfield" and "farfield" are less clear than separated forms, and the missing study design reduces informativeness.
